range: ASM-2050..ASM-2059 (free range verified this tick: `grep -c ASM-205[0-9] registry/assumptions.jsonl` = 0; registry max existing = ASM-2112. Chosen at ASM-2050 per the build brief to avoid the E0 (2043-2049, and 2100-2112) and KaE (2034-2042) ranges in flight. Increment-1 = 2050-2055; increment-2 (BFO bridge + Wikidata plan) = 2056-2059.)
purpose: SCALE-1 S1 (100k rung) candidate assumptions: increment-1 multi-source concept census + UFO-typing-yield probe (2050-2055), and increment-2 BFO-bridge lift + Wikidata domain-balance plan (2056-2059), over the local WordNet/OBO/SUMO shards. Emitted by the poc/scale build agent (owner role: builder-scale-s1) for coordinator registration; NOT written to registry/assumptions.jsonl by the build agent.

assumptions.claim: The local WordNet ∪ OBO ∪ SUMO shards yield 207,733 type-level clusters as an UNMERGED union upper bound (WordNet non-instance 110,049 + OBO classes 95,201 + SUMO classes 2,483, from raw records WN 117,791 + OBO 96,192 + SUMO 3,705 terms). This clears the 100k rung with headroom, whereas the S0 SemCor tag_cnt selection rule floored at 27,210 (ASM-1780). CONSEQUENCE: the selection-rule-exhaustion blocker (S0 interpretation §2.3) is retired by the portfolio. CAVEAT (load-bearing): this is a PRE-DEDUP upper bound; cross-source duplicates (e.g. WordNet 'cell' ↔ CL cell) are not merged, so the true post-crosswalk type-level count is LOWER and remains uncomputed (S1 step-3 dedup engineering).
assumptions.backing_ref: poc/scale/results/scale-s1-census.json (reachability_100k); regenerate via `cd poc && npm run scale:census`, wall 2.6s sha256:61417c4993f3ff43e5be9c1105bfcbf6e936e659203f7caaa56b6b4bf6041b08

assumptions.claim: OBO source-asserted ontic_category yield (cascade step 1, is_a* closure reaching a BFO 2.0 category anchor): 54,018 / 95,201 OBO classes = 56.74%, distributed disposition 32,048 (MONDO diseases), object 18,948 (UBERON anatomy 14,974 + CL cells 3,325 + PO 1,659), mode 1,929 (PATO qualities), region 691, event 385, quality 13, role 2, proposition 2. This is NONZERO on a field WordNet-only gave 0% (S0 interpretation §2.1). CONSEQUENCE: the multi-source portfolio has an EVIDENTIAL PATH to source-asserted ontic_category. This is a YIELD, NOT a PRECISION claim (correctness at the §4.3 0.95 bar needs a human audit not performed here).

OBO identity-provider-candidate yield via genus-differentia logical definitions: 24,693 / 95,201 OBO classes = 25.94% carry a genus (identity-provider candidate via the genus chain); WordNet supplies none (0%). This measured count is within 0.5% of the design's independently-cited 24,578 genus-differentia definitions (large-kernel-scale-track.md §1.1) — a cross-check that the extraction and the closure agree. OBO RO 'relationship' dependence-candidate yield: 46,755 / 95,201 = 49.11% carry ≥1 RO relationship edge (part_of/has_part/... → dependence candidate), vs WordNet 0% (antonym/similarTo are lexical, not dependence).

The BFO-reached fraction (56.74%) is a FLOOR set by the LOCAL extraction, not a ceiling: GO (38,256 records, 40% of OBO) reaches 0 BFO anchors because its three roots (biological_process/molecular_function/cellular_component) carry no explicit is_a→BFO edge in the local jsonl, while MONDO does (disease is_a BFO:disposition). 40,779 OBO classes (42.83%) currently type only via the ontology-grounded fallback crosswalk (STIPULATED per-ontology root → category). CONSEQUENCE: closing the gap to full source-asserted coverage is the S1 crosswalk step (load per-ontology BFO bridges + the SUMO↔WordNet mapping), not new science; the yield RISES once bridges are loaded.

Domain-balance gap: the local structured (source-asserted-typed) mass is ENTIRELY biological (OBO biology share of the union ≈ 45.8%; all of OBO is bio; NCBITaxon is only 402 records locally so single-TAXONOMY domination is not the local risk, single-DOMAIN biology is). A design §0 domain-balanced 100k rung (count excluding single-domain domination) therefore requires a non-biological structured source with source-asserted typing — the Wikidata class subset (§3.1, CC0) — which is NOT yet local. This is the single missing ingredient for a domain-balanced (not merely 100k-sized) rung.

The KaE / F1-K coverage gate (n_min = 240 benchmark items whose concepts the kernel covers; G-lex lexical phrase→concept matcher) needs a concept→content map whose SCALE governs whether F1-K is a toy demonstration or an impactful result: the minimal first map is the ~24 probe concepts + WordNet backbone (enough to instrument the gate); a benchmark like MMLU has an open-domain concept inventory that a bio-heavy 200k union covers only sparsely, so moving F1-K from 'known-concept-subset MMLU' (a hand-picked coverage island) to 'broad MMLU coverage' needs a sense-split, domain-balanced kernel at ≥1M concepts (design §7.7 reach-and-successful-use; §10 SCALE-GROUND-1M). RESOLUTION PATH: measure F1-K's MMLU concept-coverage against (a) the current census union and (b) a domain-balanced 1M union, to quantify the coverage lift. load_bearing=false.
assumptions.backing_ref: resolution_path: run the G-lex trigger-map coverage count of MMLU pool concepts against the census union vs a projected domain-balanced 1M union; poc/glm52-probe/interpretation-fable.md §4 (n_min=240 gate); large-kernel-scale-track.md §7.7/§10
assumptions.resolution_path: Resolved by building the >=1M sense-split, domain-balanced concept->content map (docs/next/design/scale-s1-multisource-census.md steps 2-3: BFO-bridge load + Wikidata subset); until then F1-K instruments the coverage gate only on a hand-picked concept subset.

assumptions.claim: INCREMENT-2 BFO-BRIDGE LIFT: loading the pinned bridge data/onto-obo/bfo-bridge.json (recovered root->BFO edges the subset-only kot-obo-extractor dropped) raises the OBO source-asserted ontic_category yield (is_a* closure reaching a BFO 2.0 category anchor) from 54,018/95,201 = 56.74% (increment-1, no bridge) to 95,189/95,201 = 99.99% (increment-2, with bridge) -- +41,171 records bridge-recovered, only 12 reaching no anchor. This directly confirms ASM-2053's prediction that the 56.74% was a FLOOR that rises once bridges are loaded. With-bridge category distribution: disposition 42,106 (MONDO disease 32k + GO molecular_function 10k), event 24,555 (GO biological_process + OGMS/OBI), object 23,476 (UBERON 15k + GO cellular_component 4k + CL 3.3k + CHEBI + NCBITaxon + PO), proposition 2,417 (SO sequence features), mode 1,929 (PATO), region 691, quality 13, role 2. YIELD not PRECISION (correctness at the 4.3 0.95 bar still needs the human audit).
assumptions.backing_ref: poc/scale/results/scale-s1-census.json field .contentHash = sha256:d143d90f8518fd3d4505398a680fef333e1eda6332fefcca991e311177d0c8b3 (STABLE reproducible hash over the measured payload, excludes volatile date/wallSeconds); path typing_yield_vs_wordnet_zero.obo.ontic_source_asserted_via_bfo_chain.bridge_lift; regenerate via `cd poc && npm run scale:census`, wall 3.6s

assumptions.claim: The BFO bridge table (data/onto-obo/bfo-bridge.json, 14 rows: 12 root_bridges + 2 ontology_orphan_fallback) is LIT-BACKED per row EXCEPT two STIPULATED rows: each root->BFO mapping (GO biological_process->process, GO molecular_function->realizable-entity/function, GO cellular_component->independent-continuant, SO sequence-features->generically-dependent-continuant, IAO ICE/data-item->gdc, OBI planned-process->process, CHEBI->material-entity) is the named ontology's own published BFO commitment in its canonical OWL, dropped by the local extractor and here RECOVERED (hence the reached records become source-asserted, cascade step 1). Two rows are STIPULATED with rationale: MONDO:0021178 injury->BFO:disposition (mapped for consistency with MONDO's disease treatment; the local subset lacks the disambiguating axiom) and NCBITaxon orphans->material-entity (taxa-as-Kinds per design 0; NCBITaxon asserts no BFO parent). One acknowledged coarseness: GO cellular_component->object collapses the immaterial-entity minority (e.g. membranes-as-sites) to object.
assumptions.backing_ref: data/onto-obo/bfo-bridge.json sha256:1151d68e4f67fb1be6d7e3ec48bb1cd677ac3c71f755f18cc5a0533dcccebef5; provenance per row is each source ontology's canonical OWL BFO import (GO/SO/IAO/OBI/CHEBI OBO Foundry bridge files)
assumptions.rationale: Curated 14-row BFO bridge table, sha256-pinned; provenance is each source ontologys canonical OWL BFO import (OBO Foundry bridge files, GO/SO/IAO/OBI/CHEBI). Tagged STIPULATED (conservative fallback) rather than LIT-BACKED because the source artifacts are ontology releases, not papers with a DOI/year; coordinator governance-conformance retag pending Fable confirmation.

assumptions.claim: The 12 OBO classes reaching NO BFO anchor even after the bridge are legitimately un-anchorable, not a coverage defect: BFO 2 (the BFO:0000001 entity root itself + one top class, which sit ABOVE the category anchors by construction), RO 3 (relation-ontology classes, relational not categorial), UBERON 3 (2 obsolete 'pudendum' classes + 1), OGMS 4 (dangle to IAO/OBI intermediates not covered by the 12 root bridges). CONSEQUENCE: OBO UFO ontic_category source-assertion is effectively saturated locally at 99.99%; the residual 0.01% needs no further bridge work and is correctly reported (not silently dropped).
assumptions.resolution_path: Non-load-bearing (residual is 0.01%); if ever needed, add IAO/OBI intermediate rows to the bridge for the 4 OGMS classes and skip obsolete UBERON classes.

assumptions.claim: WIKIDATA DOMAIN-BALANCE PLAN (scope only, not run this tick): a benchmark-blind slice of Wikidata's CC0 class layer (items participating in P279 subclass-of structure; named individuals excluded) targeting ~50,000-100,000 NON-BIOLOGICAL reusable classes would drop the biology share of a ~110k+ typed-structured core from the measured ~100% (all-OBO) toward ~45-65%, satisfying design 0's exclude-single-domain-domination test that the current 207,733-record union fails. UFO typing via a pinned Wikidata-root->UFO table (P279* reaching physical-object/occurrence/profession/organization/quality roots; analogous to the BFO bridge). Decontamination against the existing union is an EXACT external-ID join (P8814 WordNet-3.1-synset, P686 ChEBI, P685 NCBI-taxon, P5270 MONDO, UBERON/CL xrefs), finally yielding a real design-3.5 exactly_crosswalked_clusters count. Cost: ~1-5 GB filtered dump, ~2-8 CPU-h extract, ~$0-20, no GPU; census re-run ~4s. Added-concept counts are planning bands to be replaced by WDQS count-only queries (seconds, no dump) before any ingest commit.
assumptions.backing_ref: resolution_path: run WDQS count-only queries per class-root subtree to replace the ~50-100k band with measured counts; then acquire filtered dump + exact-ID decontamination + census re-run. Plan: docs/next/design/scale-s1-wikidata-domain-balance-plan.md; property IDs are stable Wikidata properties (P279/P31/P8814/P686/P685/P5270).
assumptions.resolution_path: Measure with WDQS count queries (no download), then execute the filtered-dump acquisition + typing + decontamination in docs/next/design/scale-s1-wikidata-domain-balance-plan.md; until measured, the ~50-100k added-concept and ~45-65% biology-share figures are planning bands, never premises.
